researchVerification: Confirmed Sylvanas as Ranger-General and Farstrider commander in the pre-Third-War window; the chapter's command posture is canon-aligned.
Confirmed Jaina as Antonidas's apprentice in the correct young-mage age band; the practical field presentation stays consistent with that profile.
Confirmed Quel'Thalas/Eversong as warded, season-held territory; the ward-hum, runestones, and border feel are grounded.
Confirmed Lor'themar as Sylvanas's Farstrider subordinate; the dry shorthand and political handoff fit the lore.

examples: Lines 27-31: folded the patrol cleanup and remount into a single breathing unit so the prose sounds less chopped than the style target allows.
Line 65: rebuilt the road paragraph so Sylvanas's environmental scan lands as one tactical notice-pattern instead of a pile of detached fragments.
Line 83: recast the runestone excuse with an em-dash to match the target's pivot rhythm.
Lines 97-99: merged the runner description into one sentence so the entrance reads as urgent field prose, not note-card listing.
Lines 367-391: reshaped the holding-room description into denser close-third observation, preserving Sylvanas's clipped mind without breaking the sentence music.
observations: Opening kill sequence already matched the target's hard, close-third combat pressure; I left the first exchange largely intact.
Lor'themar/Sylvanas banter carried the expected dry procedural wit and did not need tonal correction.
The chapter's main drift was paragraph shape: too many isolated fragment paragraphs for a style source that still wants connective tissue.
Punctuation was mostly consistent with the style target; the main missing tool was the em-dash pivot, not a formatting mismatch.
Narrative register stayed in Sylvanas's field vocabulary throughout; the fixes were about cadence, not diction replacement.
Tested the opening hunt paragraphs: all threat reads stay inside Sylvanas's footing/range/tactical filter.
Checked Lor'themar banter passages for narrator seep; the prose reports only what Sylvanas hears, sees, and infers.
Checked the runner report and border ride for impossible knowledge; the chapter never claims certainty about the mage before evidence arrives.
Checked Cyndia's briefing for emotional labeling of others; the narration stays with visible behavior, rank judgment, and Sylvanas's inferences.
Checked the Jaina reveal carefully: 'braced for disbelief' and 'furious enough to hide it' remain Sylvanas's read of posture and affect, not a head-hop into Jaina.

notes: Chapter 1 has no prior-chapter continuity burden and no requiredMemory files; all first-introduction detail is permitted.
Sylvanas, Lor'themar, Cyndia, and Jaina naming remains consistent with plan and character sheets.
observations: Verified chapter-start status against the plan: no prior chapter, so no callback obligations or bridge residue required.
Jaina's first visual read uses plan-approved first-introduction detail rather than accidental reintroduction.
Lor'themar exits north exactly as the handoff rules require, with the patrol leaving with him.
Cyndia remains active at the outpost as required for the chapter-2 handoff.
Border geography, ward behavior, and Thalassian horse handling all remain consistent with the project location notes.

examples: Lines 27-31: removed a fragment pile by joining cleanup, sweep, and remount into one longer cadence.
Line 65: converted a string of detached sensory fragments into a colon-led tactical list.
Line 83: fused four short pulses into one sentence with a hinge and em-dash.
Lines 188-191: merged 'They went hard' into the ride paragraph so movement carries through without a dead stop.
Lines 391-405: consolidated the holding-room reveal into fewer, more various sentences while keeping Sylvanas's clipped pressure.
observations: Before editing, the chapter was heavily telegrammed: short declaratives stacked faster than the style guide's floor allows.
The biggest deficit was connective tissue, especially participial phrasing and compound movement inside descriptive runs.
Dialogue scenes still run short by design, but narration now carries more of the clause-linked texture the target expects.
Em-dash and semicolon usage rose into range; participial density remains the weakest metric and will likely be the next audit target.
The revised prose sounds less like isolated note beats and more like a sustained reading voice.

examples: Lines 233-237: tied 'At the border, she went exact' directly to the human intrusion so the outpost arrival carries the same pressure instead of resetting.
observations: Beat chain from patrol ease to runner interruption already followed the plan's hard-interruption intent cleanly.
Lor'themar's departure and Sylvanas's southbound isolation remain causally linked on the page.
The ride to the outpost now carries anger into hard focus without a needless emotional reset.
Cyndia's report leads straight into the holding room, preserving the containment-pressure the plan asked for.
Chapter close still lands on Sylvanas's interrogation demand without replaying or softening the hook.

examples: Lines 317-325: tightened the satchel inventory so it reads as live scrutiny instead of a stacked evidence list.
observations: Re-read scene openings: the chapter already opens in committed action and does not throat-clear with weather or waking.
Checked attribution over-explanation around Lor'themar and Cyndia; tags stay lean and the dialogue carries its own pressure.
Checked for biography insertion around Jaina's reveal; the chapter gives only first-sight evidence and one necessary identity anchor.
Checked repeated structural frames ('No X. No Y. No Z.'); some remain in dialogue where urgency justifies them, but narration was trimmed where the stack weakened flow.
Meta-audited the runner scene, the satchel inspection, and the holding-room reveal as the chapter's weakest stretches; all three now read cleaner, though texture remains lean.
Checked every Jaina-adjacent report line for bureaucratic or clinical spill; 'rot,' 'lines,' and 'ward-stones' stay plain enough for speech.
Lor'themar's political dialogue stays dry and aristocratic without tipping into memo language.
Cyndia's briefing uses ranger shorthand and concrete observations rather than institutional jargon.
Sylvanas's questions remain pressure-tests, not exposition devices, which keeps her spoken register true to the voice sheet.
No spoken line drifted into banned terms like 'vector,' 'actionable,' 'optimal,' or other report-voice contaminants.

textureMetrics.note: Structural texture improved materially, but participial density remains below the style-guide floor and short-sentence density remains high enough that a texture revision loop would be justified if the style-auditor demands it.

metaAudit.conclusion: Chapter is now cleaner and more continuous in the mouth. The remaining weakness is texture, not voice or continuity.
